input.headline: Kids do not want to go to child-free weddings either

raw.headline: Kids do not want to go to child-free weddings either

winner: At the child-free wedding, when a toddler snuck in and started eating cake, the guests all gasped—until they realized the kid was the *only* one having fun.

candidates.text: Why did the kid bring a ladder to the wedding? Because he heard there was no place for children... *literally*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with 'literally' adds clever double meaning, concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: I tried to attend a child-free wedding, but my toddler showed up anyway — turns out, he's a real party pooper.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a relatable situation, concise and specific, uses wordplay effectively."}

candidates.text: I told my kid I was going to a wedding, and he said, "Can we bring snacks? I don't want to go to a party where nobody wants to be around kids.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a relatable, specific scenario and clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: I tried to attend a child-free wedding with my toddler, and now I'm banned from all future events — apparently, my kid's just really bad at being child-free.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with specific, relatable scenario and clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: At the child-free wedding, when a toddler snuck in and started eating cake, the guests all gasped—until they realized the kid was the *only* one having fun.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: Kids these days—no ring, no wedding, just a bunch of them texting under the table about why they’d rather be at a kid’s birthday party where the cake is definitely bigger than any adult’s ego.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with specific, relatable scenario and clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: The bride was furious when her guests complained about the child-free rule, until she realized they weren't protesting the lack of kids—they were just annoyed they had to be the only adults responsible for their own behavior.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with clear relevance to the headline, concise and specific, uses natural language effectively."}

candidates.text: Even the children are starting to find child-free weddings a bit too "parent-al.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Pun on 'parent-al' is clever and specific, aligns well with the headline, and is concise."}

candidates.text: The children refused to attend the child-free wedding because they were told they weren't allowed to bring their imaginary friends, and they didn't want to be caught in a lie.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on the concept of child-free weddings."}
